documents_used.name: SLA for Microsoft Online Services (consolidated; incl. Azure compute/GPU)
Microsoft Product Terms for Online Services

availability_definition.value: instance-level and cluster/throughput-based (varies by service); VM in Availability Zones 99.99%, Availability Set/Dedicated Host Group 99.95%, Single-Instance VM 99.9% (Premium SSD); AKS API server 99.95% (AZ) / 99.9% (non-AZ); Capacity Blocks capacity-availability based
availability_definition.citation: Uptime Calculation and Service Levels for Virtual Machines in Availability Zones

source.name: SLA for Microsoft Online Services (consolidated; incl. Azure compute/GPU)

credit_regime.value: Tiered by service; e.g. VM Availability Zones: <99.99%=10%, <99%=25%, <95%=100%. Standard tier VMs <99.9%=10%, <99%=25%, <95%=100%. Max credit 100% of monthly fees for affected resource. Service Credits are sole and exclusive remedy.
credit_regime.citation: Service Credits are your sole and exclusive remedy

source.name: SLA for Microsoft Online Services (consolidated; incl. Azure compute/GPU)

claim_mechanics.value: Customer must submit a claim to Microsoft customer support with detailed description, time/duration of downtime, affected resource names, number/location of affected users, and error description. For Azure, claim must be received within 60 days of the Incident.
claim_mechanics.citation: we must receive the claim within 60 days of the Incident

source.name: SLA for Microsoft Online Services (consolidated; incl. Azure compute/GPU)

sla_exclusions.value: Present: force majeure/outside reasonable control; external network/ISP/third-party; single-datacenter non-geo-resilient; customer fault/misuse; beta/preview/trial; throttling/abuse; exceeding quotas; scheduled/maintenance windows; customer-initiated operations (restart, scale). Performance degradation without unavailability excluded.
sla_exclusions.citation: This SLA and any applicable Service Levels do not apply to any performance or…

source.name: SLA for Microsoft Online Services (consolidated; incl. Azure compute/GPU)

capacity_reservation.value: Public docs describe On Demand Capacity Reservations for VMs and Capacity Blocks (reservation of compute capacity for a specified duration), each with their own SLA. Broader enterprise commitment vehicles (e.g. Enterprise Agreement pricing/commitments) negotiated, not published. Cancellation may incur fee per Product Terms.
capacity_reservation.citation: Capacity Block means a reservation of compute capacity for a specified duration

source.name: SLA for Microsoft Online Services (consolidated; incl. Azure compute/GPU)

data_use_ai_training.value: Does not train on customer data by default: Microsoft Generative AI Services will not use Customer Data to train any generative AI foundation model except per Customer's documented instructions. Output Content is Customer Data; Microsoft does not own it.
data_use_ai_training.citation: will not use Customer Data to train any generative AI foundation model

source.name: Microsoft Product Terms for Online Services

suspension_rights.value: Grounds: material breach (non-payment), AUP/Code of Conduct violation, security risk, trade-law risk. 30 days' notice before suspension normally, but immediate suspension if payment declined or to prevent unauthorized access / protect systems. AUP violations suspended only to extent reasonably necessary with reasonable notice unless immediate needed.
suspension_rights.citation: Microsoft will give Customer 30 days' notice before such suspension unless

termination.value: Termination for convenience: either party on 60 days' notice. Post-termination data retrieval: for non-renewed Online Services, access continues 30 days after expiration; disabled account gives Customer 90 days to extract Customer Data.
termination.citation: Either party may terminate this Agreement without cause on 60 days' notice

unilateral_modification.value: Microsoft may update the Agreement. General Terms/other terms: at least 60 days' notice, effective on renewal. SLA: at least 90 days' notice for adverse material changes. Product Terms: Material Adverse Changes apply at renewal; other changes effective on posting. No changes during initial subscription term for SLA.
unilateral_modification.citation: We will provide at least 90 days' notice for adverse material changes to this…

source.name: SLA for Microsoft Online Services (consolidated; incl. Azure compute/GPU)

governing_law_disputes.value: Governing law: State of Washington and US federal law. Venue: if Customer sues (non-Europe Affiliate) King County, WA; if Microsoft sues, Customer's HQ; Europe = Ireland. Binding arbitration: no (court venues specified). Class-action waiver: not specified.
governing_law_disputes.citation: governed by and construed in accordance with the laws of the State of Washington
